history.content:
<thought>
I am at row 02 column B facing right. The cell directly ahead at row 02 column C is lava, so moving forward would be unsafe. A safe route to the goal at row 04 column D is to go down through row 03. To start that route, I should turn right to face down.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 02 column B facing down. The cell directly ahead at row 03 column B is a safe floor cell. The goal is at row 04 column D, and moving forward brings me closer while avoiding the lava at row 02 column C and row 04 column C.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 03 column B facing down. The cell directly ahead at row 04 column B is safe floor. The goal is at row 04 column D, and moving forward now is a safe step that brings me closer while avoiding lava at row 04 column C.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 04 column B facing down. The cell directly ahead would be row 05 column B, which is a wall, so I should not move forward. The goal is at row 04 column D, and there is lava at row 04 column C, so I cannot go directly right across row 04. A safe route is to go up first through row 03 column B, then move across row 03 toward column D. To begin, I need to face right? No, from facing down, the first safe move upward requires turning around eventually, but the immediate useful step is to turn right to face left or turn left to face right; neither allows safe forward progress because both side cells are walls/lava paths later. Since the intended route starts by going up, I should first reorient from down toward up; the first turn can be either left or right. I will turn right as the first step.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 04 column B facing left (`<`). The cell in front of me at row 04 column A is a wall `#`, so moving forward is not possible. The goal is at row 04 column D, but row 04 column C is lava `L`, so I should not go directly right through it. A safe route is to go up through row 03 and then across to column D before going down to the goal. First, I need to turn to face up.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 04 column B facing up. The goal is at row 04 column D, but row 04 column C is lava, so I cannot go directly right across. A safe route is to go up through row 03 column B and row 02 column B, then turn right and move across to column D, then go down to the goal. The immediate safe step is to move forward to row 03 column B.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 03 column B facing up. The goal is at row 04 column D. Moving forward would hit a wall at row 02 column B? Actually row 02 column B is open, but going upward does not help reach the goal efficiently. A safe path is to go from B3 to C3 to D3 to D4, avoiding the lava at C2 and C4. So I should first face right.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 03 column B facing right. The goal is at row 04 column D. Moving forward to row 03 column C is safe, and it advances me toward the goal while avoiding the lava at row 04 column C.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 03 column C facing right. The cell directly ahead at row 03 column D is a safe floor cell. The goal is at row 04 column D, so moving forward brings me closer without entering lava.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 03 column D, facing right (`>`). The cell directly in front of me is row 03 column E, which is a wall (`#`), so moving forward is not possible. The goal is at row 04 column D, directly below me. To move toward it, I should turn right to face down.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 03 column D, facing down. The cell directly ahead at row 04 column D is the goal G, which is safe to enter and reaches the objective immediately.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
